title: Open Calls for Papers, Proposals, and Applications
gist: This is an NBER administrative listing of open calls for papers and proposals, not a research paper. The slate of topics NBER is soliciting signals where the academic-economics research agenda is heading: AI in financial markets, tariffs, long-term deficits, and emerging-market fragmentation.

themes.theme: Research agenda signals from open calls

insights.claim: NBER has an open call for papers on 'AI, Digitization, and Financial Markets' (closing June 17, 2026), signaling institutional research focus on AI's impact on finance.
insights.speaker: NBER
insights.so_what: Forthcoming NBER work on AI in financial markets is worth tracking for early academic evidence on AI-driven market structure shifts.
insights.quote: AI, Digitization, and Financial Markets (call closes June 17, 2026)

insights.claim: NBER is soliciting papers on 'Tariffs and Economic Performance' (closing December 16, 2026) and 'Long-Term Budget Deficits: Economic Effects and Potential Responses' (closing August 31, 2026), indicating heavy research focus on trade protectionism and fiscal sustainability.
insights.speaker: NBER
insights.so_what: Tariffs and long-term deficits are the macro themes NBER expects sustained academic output on — both are direct rates/inflation watch items.
insights.quote: Tariffs and Economic Performance (call closes December 16, 2026)

insights.claim: NBER has an open call on 'Emerging Markets: Navigating a Fragmented Global Economy' (closing September 8, 2026), reflecting institutional attention to geoeconomic fragmentation.
insights.speaker: NBER
insights.so_what: Confirms fragmentation/de-risking as a durable research theme relevant to EM allocation.
insights.quote: Emerging Markets: Navigating a Fragmented Global Economy (call closes September 8, 2026)
